name: Tui - Monte Aloia Tour - Monte Aloia Natural Park
name_original: Tui - Monte Aloia Vuelta - Parque Natural Monte Aloia
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Tui are characterized by diverse landscapes, ranging from the banks of the Miño River to the elevated terrain of Monte Aloia Natural Park. The region features rolling hills of Galicia and offers cross-border cycling experiences into Portugal. Routes include a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, with varied elevation gains suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Tui The most popular touring cycling route is Tui - Monte Aloia Tour - Monte Aloia Natural Park, a 13.6 miles (21.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain through the natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ecovia do Rio Lima – Ponte de Fillaboa loop from Caldelas de Tui, a moderate 23.2 miles (37.3 km) path. This route features mostly paved surfaces and offers a moderate cycling experience. Local touring cyclists also love the Roman Bridge of Pontenova loop from Tui, a 19.0 miles (30.5 km) trail leading through historic connections along the Miño River, often completed in about 1 hour 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Tui is defined by the Miño River, Monte Aloia Natural Park, and historic connections. The network includes options from moderate loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Tui are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tui's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Tui?
A: The touring cycling routes around Tui offer a diverse mix of terrain. You'll find paths with mostly paved surfaces, ideal for leisure rides, as well as segments that are unpaved and more challenging. This variety allows for different cycling preferences, from smooth journeys along the Miño River to more adventurous rides through areas like Monte Aloia Natural Park.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those seeking an easier ride in Tui?
A: While many routes around Tui feature significant elevation, there are options for less experienced touring cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33711688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tui – Ponte de Fillaboa loop from Caldelas de Tui&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 23.2-mile (37.3 km) path with mostly paved surfaces, offering a more gentle cycling experience.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders in Tui?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Tui offers several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950540860/tui-monte-aloia-tour-monte-aloia-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tui - Monte Aloia Tour - Monte Aloia Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.6-mile (21.9 km) trail with significant elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40365743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Orbenlle – Baiona Marina loop from Tui&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 58.6 miles (94.3 km) and includes substantial climbs.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Tui?
A: Yes, Tui features several circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40370610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tui – Santa María dos Anjos loop from Tui&lt;/a&gt;, a 34.7-mile (55.8 km) trail through varied terrain. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33711688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tui – Ponte de Fillaboa loop from Caldelas de Tui&lt;/a&gt;, offering a moderate experience.

Q: What natural attractions or viewpoints can I discover along the cycling routes in Tui?
A: The touring cycling routes around Tui offer access to several natural attractions and viewpoints. You can explore the scenic Monte Aloia Natural Park, which many routes traverse. For stunning vistas, consider highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3792978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Alto da Groba&lt;/a&gt; or challenging climbs such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1679056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Alto da Groba via Mougás&lt;/a&gt;. The Miño River also provides picturesque scenery along its banks.

Q: Are there any historic landmarks or points of interest accessible by touring bike from Tui?
A: Tui's strategic location offers access to several historic landmarks. Routes often lead to cross-border experiences, such as those connecting to Valença Fortress in Portugal. You can also find routes that pass by the Roman Bridge of Orbenlle. For a blend of history and nature, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24308055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María dos Anjos – Valença Fortress 🏰 loop from O Eido do Carballo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about their experience in Tui?
A: The touring cycling routes around Tui are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from the Miño River banks to the elevated Monte Aloia Natural Park, and the opportunities for cross-border adventures.

Q: Can I find accommodation options for cyclists near the routes in Tui?
A: Yes, there are several accommodation options suitable for cyclists in and around Tui. These include various huts and albergues, which are often popular with pilgrims and touring cyclists. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4450710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albergue Quinta Estrada Romana&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2889263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;São Teotónio Hostel&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5041015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albergue Casa Alternativo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Tui?
A: The region around Tui generally offers pleasant conditions for touring cycling across multiple seasons. The varied terrain and natural beauty can be enjoyed in spring and autumn for milder temperatures, while summer provides longer daylight hours. Winter cycling is also possible, though conditions may vary, especially on higher elevation routes.

Q: Are there opportunities for cross-border touring cycling from Tui into Portugal?
A: Absolutely. Tui's location on the Miño River, which forms a natural border with Portugal, provides excellent opportunities for cross-border touring cycling. Routes can lead to destinations like Valença Fortress in Portugal, allowing you to experience both Spanish and Portuguese landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24308055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María dos Anjos – Valença Fortress 🏰 loop from O Eido do Carballo&lt;/a&gt; is one such example.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Tui?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes available around Tui, catering to various skill levels. This includes a mix of moderate and difficult trails, ensuring there's something for every touring cyclist looking to explore the region's diverse landscapes.
tips: The south side of the plaza is dominated by the Igrexa de San Francisco. Originally it was part of the Franciscan convent of San Antonio, built between 1682 and 1728, with an outstanding Baroque altarpiece from 1741 (the work of Portuguese artists), side altarpieces, a half-orange dome and a large choir. On the south side is the Chapel of the "Venerable Third Order", built in 1777. The former convent is now the diocesan minor seminary, since 1850, with two cloisters, one of them Baroque and open.

Its origin is traditionally attributed to Roman times, linked to the Via XIX, Most of the modern structure are from medieval and modern times. Its Roman origin cannot be ruled out even if it was reformed in later times as several cariological sites were found in the area with military pieces and other Roman artifacts.
